heading: FLAME Wildfire Suppression Reserve Funds
label: 1748a FLAME Wildfire Suppression Reserve Funds
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Federal Land Assistance, Management, and Enhancement Act of 2009, also known as the FLAME Act of 2009, and also as part of the Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2010, and not as part of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 which comprises this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: public land, as defined in ;section 1702 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: units of the National Park System;

children.label: C
children.text: refuges of the National Wildlife Refuge System;

children.label: D
children.text: land held in trust by the United States for the benefit of Indian tribes or members of an Indian tribe; and

children.label: E
children.text: land in the National Forest System, as defined in .section 1609(a) of title 16

children.heading: FLAME Fund
children.label: 2 FLAME Fund
children.text: The term “FLAME Fund” means a FLAME Wildfire Suppression Reserve Fund established by subsection (b).

children.heading: Relevant congressional committees
children.label: 3 Relevant congressional committees
children.text: The term “relevant congressional committees” means the Committee on Appropriations, the Committee on Natural Resources, and the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Appropriations, the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources, and the Committee on Indian Affairs of the Senate.

children.label: i
children.text: Federal land described in subparagraphs (A), (B), (C), and (D) of paragraph (1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the FLAME Fund established for the Department of the Interior; and

children.label: i
children.text: National Forest System land; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the FLAME Fund established for the Department of the Agriculture.
The FLAME Wildfire Suppression Reserve Fund for the Department of the Interior.
The FLAME Wildfire Suppression Reserve Fund for the Department of Agriculture.

children.heading: Purpose of FLAME Funds
children.label: c Purpose of FLAME Funds
children.text: The FLAME Funds shall be available to cover the costs of large or complex wildfire events and as a reserve when amounts provided for wildfire suppression and Federal emergency response in the Wildland Fire Management appropriation accounts are exhausted.

children.label: A
children.text: Such amounts as are appropriated to that FLAME Fund.

children.label: B
children.text: Such amounts as are transferred to that FLAME Fund under paragraph (5).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: A Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the FLAME Funds such amounts as are necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Congressional intent
children.label: B Congressional intent
children.text: It is the intent of Congress that, for fiscal year 2011 and each fiscal year thereafter, the amounts requested by the President for a FLAME Fund should be not less than the amount estimated by the Secretary concerned as the amount necessary for that fiscal year for wildfire suppression activities of the Secretary that meet the criteria specified in subsection (e)(2)(B)(i).

children.heading: Sense of Congress on designation of flame fund appropriations, supplemental funding request, and supplement to other suppression funding
children.label: C Sense of Congress on designation of flame fund appropriations, supplemental funding request, and supplement to other suppression funding
i
children.text: amounts appropriated to a FLAME Fund in excess of the amount estimated by the Secretary concerned as the amount necessary for that fiscal year for wildfire suppression activities of the Secretary that meet the criteria specified in subsection (e)(2)(B)(i) should be designated as amounts necessary to meet emergency needs;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary concerned should promptly make a supplemental request for additional funds to replenish the FLAME Fund if the Secretary determines that the FLAME Fund will be exhausted within 30 days; and

children.label: iii
children.text: funding made available through the FLAME Fund should be used to supplement the funding otherwise appropriated to the Secretary concerned for wildfire suppression and Federal emergency response in the Wildland Fire Management appropriation accounts.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 3 Availability
children.text: Amounts in a FLAME Fund shall remain available to the Secretary concerned until expended.

children.heading: Notice of insufficient funds
children.label: 4 Notice of insufficient funds
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall notify the relevant congressional committees if the Secretary estimates that only 60 days worth of funds remain in the FLAME Fund administered by that Secretary.

children.heading: Transfer authority
children.label: 5 Transfer authority
children.text: If a FLAME Fund has insufficient funds, the Secretary concerned administering the other FLAME Fund may transfer amounts to the FLAME Fund with insufficient funds. Not more than $100,000,000 may be transferred from a FLAME Fund during any fiscal year under this authority.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (2) and (3), amounts in a FLAME Fund shall be available to the Secretary concerned to transfer to the Wildland Fire Management appropriation account of that Secretary to pay the costs of wildfire suppression activities of that Secretary that are separate from amounts for wildfire suppression activities annually appropriated to that Secretary under the Wildland Fire Management appropriation account of that Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Amounts in a FLAME Fund shall be available for transfer under paragraph (1) only after that Secretary concerned issues a declaration that a wildfire suppression event is eligible for funding from the FLAME Fund.

children.label: I
children.text: the fire covers 300 or more acres; or

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary concerned determines that the fire has required an emergency Federal response based on the significant complexity, severity, or threat posed by the fire to human life, property, or resources; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the cumulative costs of wildfire suppression and Federal emergency response activities for the Secretary concerned will exceed, within 30 days, all of the amounts previously appropriated (including amounts appropriated under an emergency designation, but excluding amounts appropriated to the FLAME Fund) to the Secretary concerned for wildfire suppression and Federal emergency response.

children.heading: State, private, and tribal land
children.label: 3 State, private, and tribal land
children.text: Use of a FLAME Fund for emergency wildfire suppression activities on State land, private land, and tribal land shall be consistent with any existing agreements in which the Secretary concerned has agreed to assume responsibility for wildfire suppression activities on the land.

children.heading: Treatment of anticipated and predicted activities
children.label: f Treatment of anticipated and predicted activities
children.text: For fiscal year 2011 and subsequent fiscal years, the Secretary concerned shall request funds within the Wildland Fire Management appropriation account of that Secretary for regular wildfire suppression activities that do not meet the criteria specified in subsection (e)(2)(B)(i).

children.heading: Prohibition on other transfers
children.label: g Prohibition on other transfers
children.text: The Secretary concerned may not transfer funds from non-fire accounts to the Wildland Fire Management appropriation account of that Secretary unless amounts in the FLAME Fund of that Secretary and any amounts appropriated to that Secretary for the purpose of wildfire suppression will be exhausted within 30 days.

children.heading: Accounting and reporting requirements
children.label: 1 Accounting and reporting requirements
children.text: The Secretary concerned shall account and report on amounts transferred from the respective FLAME Fund in a manner that is consistent with existing National Fire Plan reporting procedures.

children.label: A
children.text: describes the obligation and expenditure of amounts transferred from the FLAME Fund; and

children.label: B
children.text: includes any recommendations that the Secretary concerned may have to improve the administrative control and oversight of the FLAME Fund.

children.heading: Estimates of wildfire suppression costs to improve budgeting and funding
children.label: 3 Estimates of wildfire suppression costs to improve budgeting and funding

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Consistent with the schedule provided in subparagraph (C), the Secretary concerned shall submit to the relevant congressional committees an estimate of anticipated wildfire suppression costs for the applicable fiscal year.

children.heading: Independent review
children.label: B Independent review
children.text: The methodology for developing the estimates under subparagraph (A) shall be subject to periodic independent review to ensure compliance with subparagraph (D).

children.label: i
children.text: the first week of March of each year;

children.label: ii
children.text: the first week of May of each year;

children.label: iii
children.text: the first week of July of each year; and

children.label: iv
children.text: if a bill making appropriations for the Department of the Interior and the Forest Service for the following fiscal year has not been enacted by September 1, the first week of September of each year.

children.label: i
children.text: climate, weather, and other relevant data; and

children.label: ii
children.text: models and other analytic tools.

children.heading: Termination of authority
children.label: i Termination of authority
children.text: The authority of the Secretary concerned to use the FLAME Fund established for that Secretary shall terminate at the end of the third fiscal year in which no appropriations to, or withdrawals from, that FLAME Fund have been made for a period of three consecutive fiscal years. Upon termination of such authority, any amounts remaining in the affected FLAME Fund shall be transferred to, and made a part of, the Wildland Fire Management appropriation account of the Secretary concerned for wildland suppression activities.
